meta.collectorsTitle: MANCHA — For collectors. Hand-picked emerging art.
meta.collectorsDesc: Collect hand-picked emerging artists before the world discovers them. Few per season. When it closes, it closes for good.
meta.artistsTitle: MANCHA — For artists. Apply for access.
meta.artistsDesc: We choose emerging artists by hand. No metrics, no followers. Just the work. Apply to Season 01.
meta.aboutDesc: Why MANCHA exists: an institution that chooses before it's obvious, with its own judgement and no open catalogues.
meta.selectedDesc: The permanent registry of every artist who passed the filter. Season by season.
meta.catalogDesc: Every piece at auction this season. Explore, follow and bid on what stops you.
meta.manifestoTitle: MANCHA — What happens here leaves a mark.
meta.manifestoDesc: MANCHA's manifesto. For artists who understand that belonging to something worthwhile is an achievement, not a formality.
meta.legalDesc: How MANCHA works, what data we keep and the rules of the game. No fine print.
meta.notasTitle: MANCHA — What we think about art.
meta.notasDesc: MANCHA Editorial: the 20 most influential artists, the most expensive paintings, the great museums and what art says about the world.
meta.elegidosDesc: If you're reading this, someone thought you deserved to find it.
meta.indexDesc: The permanent record of every work MANCHA has shown. Provenance, status and authorship, season by season.
meta.beforeWorldDesc: Collecting at MANCHA isn't buying an object — it's arriving first. The manifesto for those who see before the world does.
meta.councilDesc: MANCHA's Founding Curatorial Council. The work speaks first.
meta.curatorsDesc: Who selects at MANCHA: a curatorial council that judges every work blind. The work first.

footer.seasonPre: Open call Aug 1–31 · Season 01 opens Sep 1, 2026

auth.joinSubtitle: To collect and save favorites, or to apply as an artist — upload your works now or whenever you like.
auth.bioPh: Two or three sentences about your work and what drives you to make it.
auth.worksNote: After confirming your email you enter your account and upload your works (up to 3: image, title and starting price). You can do it right away or later — your account stays active either way.
auth.techniqueHint: Pick a category or type your own medium.

picker.collectDesc: I want to explore hand-picked emerging artists and collect before the world discovers them.
picker.artistDesc: I want MANCHA to review my work and present it to collectors next season.

collectors.num1Note: We choose artists before they're obvious. When you reach MANCHA, you arrive first.
collectors.num2Note: Only three pieces per artist. No endless catalogue. Each one's best, with room to be seen properly.
collectors.num3Note: A season happens only once. The artists, the works and the moment never come together the same way again. You collect a moment, not a catalogue.
collectors.quote: "We don't sell art. We present artists someone believed in first."
collectors.manifestoP1: The difference between a collector and someone buying decorative art is judgement. At MANCHA, the judgement is already made. We review by hand, choose few, and protect the collector's attention.
collectors.manifestoP2: No algorithms, no popularity, no follower count. The work speaks first. What you find here has already passed a filter most artists do not.
collectors.step1Body: A small, curated catalogue. Few artists, few pieces. Just enough for something to stop you.
collectors.step2Body: You choose a piece and bid above the minimum. No extra fees on the buyer's side.
collectors.step3Body: If someone outbids you, you can bid again whenever you like. The season lasts three months.
collectors.step4Body: If your bid closes first, we coordinate payment and shipping directly with you by email.
collectors.piecesAvailable: {count, plural, one {# piece available} other {# pieces available}}
collectors.avisoSub: The gallery opens September 1. Leave your email and we'll let you know the moment the catalogue is ready — before anyone else.

artists.num1Note: Not an open catalogue. A hand-made selection, season by season.
artists.num2Note: Their best. No filler. Nothing left over.
artists.num3Note: Of every sale. No cost to apply or to exhibit. We only charge if there's a sale.
artists.quote: "Emerging art doesn't die from lack of talent. It dies in the noise."
artists.manifestoP1: At fairs where hundreds of works compete for a three-second glance. In profiles lost to the scroll. In portfolios no one opens.
artists.manifestoP2: MANCHA is the institutional answer to that problem. Few artists per season — the ones we choose, not the ones who managed to sign up. Each shows exactly three pieces. That restriction forces you to show your best.
artists.stmt1: We don't look at your followers.
artists.step1Body: No gallery CV, no perfectly polished portfolio. Just what you've made, in your own words.
artists.step2Body: A person, not an algorithm. If your work makes it in, we write to you directly with next steps.
artists.step3Body: Your own space for the whole season. Collectors bid live, with no middlemen.
artists.step4Body: Of every sale, straight to you. MANCHA handles the rest: shipping, communication, logistics.
artists.stepsCtaNotify: Notify me when the open call starts →
artists.avisoSubPre: Leave your email and we'll let you know the exact moment the open call opens.
artists.avisoSubConv: The open call closes August 31. Season 01 opens September 1.
artists.avisoSubActive: Once you're in MANCHA, you stay in the registry forever.

about.num1Note: Not an open catalogue. A hand-made selection, season by season.
about.num2Note: Their best. No filler. Nothing left over.
about.num3Note: When it closes, it's gone. What you see today may not be available again.
about.manifBig1: Emerging art dies in the noise.
about.manifBody1: At fairs where hundreds of works compete for a three-second glance. In profiles lost to the scroll. In portfolios no one opens. The problem isn't a lack of talent — it's a lack of judgement.
about.manifBig2: MANCHA is the institutional answer to that problem.
about.manifBody2: A season has few artists — the ones we choose, not the ones who managed to sign up. Each shows exactly three pieces. Not one more. That restriction isn't a whim: it forces you to show your best, gives each work room to truly be seen, and protects the collector's attention. The judgement is the product.
about.filterTitle1: We don't look at your followers.
about.filterP1: Every application is reviewed by a person, not an algorithm. We don't care if you have a thousand followers or none, whether you trained at an academy or taught yourself. We care about one thing: that the work has something the rest hasn't seen yet.
about.filterP2: Most applications don't make it. Not as punishment — as standard. Every artist we accept carries the MANCHA name with them.
about.pujaConvP1: Applying to MANCHA is free. We review every application personally — no automatic filters, no follower metrics. If your work makes it in, we tell you directly and guide you through uploading your pieces.
about.pujaConvP2: When Season 01 opens on September 1, collectors will be able to explore, bid and take your work home. You keep the larger share of every sale. Always.
about.pujaDefP1: Each piece has a minimum bid and is auctioned throughout the season. When it closes, the highest bidder takes the work — and pays exactly that, with no extra charges on the buyer's side.
about.pujaDefP2: No middlemen, no hidden fees, no fine print. Just the work, the price you set, and the artist who made it.
about.etymQuote: "A mancha is the first gesture on the blank canvas."
about.etymBody: Before control, before technique — pure intention. It's also what remains, what doesn't fade, what marks. The name says both at once: the beginning of something and the trace it leaves. Artists who pass through MANCHA stay in the registry. That doesn't change.
about.collectorsQuote: "You don't collect a piece. You collect a moment."
about.collectorsBody: Collecting at MANCHA isn't buying an object — it's arriving first. If you're here as a collector, this is your manifesto.

selected.sub: Every artist who entered MANCHA stays here forever. Season by season — the lineage builds from day one.

piece.soldText2: Fell for it anyway? Write to us. If there's real interest, we'll check with the artist whether there's something more to show or a way to reach an agreement.

piece.bidTrust: By bidding you commit to pay if you're the highest bidder when the season closes. We reach out by email to coordinate — no extra costs.

account.completeProfileSub: This activates your artist account. You can upload your works whenever you like afterwards.
account.bioPh: Two or three sentences about your work and what drives you to make it.

elegidos.beat2a: Each season, a group gets in.
elegidos.beat2b: Three pieces. Three months. A page that's yours alone —
elegidos.beat2c: not one more profile lost among thousands.
elegidos.beat3a: Most who apply don't get in this season.
elegidos.learnFirst: or get to know MANCHA first

misc.revisaSub: We sent you a confirmation link. As soon as you open it, your account is active.
misc.pagoSub: We'll email you in the next few hours to coordinate shipping. Any questions:
misc.welcomeBody1: MANCHA was born from a simple conviction: genuine emerging art doesn't need noise, it needs judgement. That's why each season we hand-pick a small group of artists — before they're obvious — and put them in front of the right people.
misc.welcomeBody2: You are one of those people. Thank you for being here from the start. What we build together — collectors and artists — is something worth watching grow.
misc.roleSwitchToArtist: Are you an artist? Switch profile →
misc.roleSwitchToCollector: Are you a collector? Switch profile →

manifestoPage.heroSub: MANCHA isn't a showcase. It's a decision.
manifestoPage.aperturaP1: There is too much art in the world and very few places that take seriously the task of separating what matters from what merely exists. MANCHA was born to be that place.
manifestoPage.aperturaP2: Not an algorithm. Not a vanity fair. A human eye, season after season, looking for the work that has something the rest can't yet name.
manifestoPage.quote1: "Whoever reaches MANCHA doesn't come to exhibit.
manifestoPage.sigWhatP1: An online emerging-art gallery, built on a simple premise: restriction creates value. Few artists per season. Three pieces each. Limited time. No endless catalogues, no noise.
manifestoPage.sigWhatP2: Collectors come because they trust the judgement. The judgement exists because we don't let just anyone in. That circle isn't arrogance — it's the architecture of something that lasts.
manifestoPage.sigWhereP1: We're growing. We don't hide it — we say it with pride. Each season adds new collectors, new works, new conversations about art worth having.
manifestoPage.sigWhereP2: The artists who enter now are part of the foundation. What we build together today is the reputation we all carry tomorrow. That carries a weight that doesn't fade.
manifestoPage.p1Cuerpo: Not out of exclusivity for its own sake. But because attention is finite and real work deserves real space. Each season is few artists — chosen, not selected by volume.
manifestoPage.p2Cuerpo: Being in MANCHA isn't a moment — it's a mark. The artist who passed through here carries it with them. Collectors know it. The market learns it.
manifestoPage.p3Cuerpo: 75% of every sale goes straight to the artist. No hidden fees, no fine print. We build so that art can be a real living, not a promise.
manifestoPage.p4Titulo: We grow together, or not at all.
manifestoPage.p4Cuerpo: We're in an expansion stage. Every artist who enters is an ambassador of what we're building. Your network, your voice, your work — it all adds to the weight of the name.
manifestoPage.p5Cuerpo: We don't look at followers or prizes or academies. We look at what you made. If the work has something the world hasn't seen yet, that's what matters to us.
manifestoPage.huellaP1: An artist who passed through MANCHA can take their path in any direction. What they don't lose is the record that there was a moment — a season — when someone looked at their work and said: this gets in.
manifestoPage.huellaP2: That is collectible. That's what buyers look for: the trace of someone who saw it first. And MANCHA exists to be that trace in the work's history.
manifestoPage.llamadoSub: We don't ask for perfection. We ask for work that has something. If that's you — apply. If not, save this link for when you are.
manifestoPage.firmaText: Built with judgement. Growing with intention.

collectorManifesto.heroSub: Collecting at MANCHA isn't buying an object. It's arriving first.
collectorManifesto.aperturaP1: The art that matters is almost never recognized in time. By the time everyone sees it, it's late — and expensive. MANCHA exists for the moment before: when the work is still a secret.
collectorManifesto.aperturaP2: Collecting early isn't luck. It's judgement, and being in the right place before the rest.
collectorManifesto.sigWhatP1: Work by hand-picked emerging artists, one season at a time. Few pieces, limited time. Each arrives with its provenance: where it appeared, when, and that you saw it first.
collectorManifesto.sigWhatP2: Not an endless catalogue where everything is always available. A selection that happens — and then stays on the record, for good.
collectorManifesto.sigWhereP1: Each season opens a short window. For about three months the work is on view and can be collected. Then the season is sealed and enters the Index.
collectorManifesto.sigWhereP2: What you collect keeps your name in its history. The moment passes; the provenance stays.
collectorManifesto.p1Titulo: First you see it, then you understand it.
collectorManifesto.p1Cuerpo: The best moment to collect an artist is before it's obvious. Once it's obvious, you're no longer collecting — you're buying what someone else discovered.
collectorManifesto.p2Cuerpo: The artists, the works and the moment come together only once. Even if some pieces move on, that window never returns the same.
collectorManifesto.p3Cuerpo: You don't collect by trend or by name. You collect because something stopped you. That decision, repeated with judgement, is what builds a collection that matters.
collectorManifesto.p4Cuerpo: Whoever saw it first stays in the work's history. Being among MANCHA's first collectors isn't a detail — it's part of what you collect.
collectorManifesto.huellaP1: Every work that enters your collection carries the record that you chose it, at this moment, before the world.
collectorManifesto.huellaP2: That can't be bought later. It comes only from being early.
collectorManifesto.llamadoSub: The next season opens soon. Step in before the world.

legal.sub: How MANCHA works, what we keep, and the rules of the game — explained the way we are: straight.
legal.s1p1: When you create an account, apply as an artist or join the waitlist, we keep your name, email and — depending on the case — city, bio, Instagram or portfolio.
legal.s1p2: When you bid, we keep the amount and the piece. Payment is processed securely by Stripe. MANCHA never sees or stores your card number.
legal.s2p1: To confirm your bids, let you know if you won a piece, and coordinate payment and shipping.
legal.s2p2: If you joined the waitlist, to notify you when there are new artists or the next season opens.
legal.s2p3: We don't sell your data to third parties. We may use analytics tools to understand how the site is used, without identifying you personally.
legal.s3p1: By bidding on a piece you commit to pay if you're the highest bidder when the season closes. It's not a reservation — it's a binding bid.
legal.s3p2: If you win and don't complete payment within a reasonable time, we reserve the right to offer the piece to the next highest bidder and to disable your account.
legal.s4p1: As a buyer, you pay exactly what you bid. There are no additional charges on the buyer's side — no commissions, no service fees, no surprises.
legal.s4p2: The breakdown of the sale is an internal matter between MANCHA and the artist. As a buyer, all you need to know is that what you bid is what you pay.
legal.s5p1: Shipping is coordinated by email directly with the artist once payment is confirmed, case by case.
legal.s5p2: MANCHA acts as an intermediary to facilitate coordination, but is not responsible for delays or damage that occur during transit once the piece has been dispatched.
legal.s6p1: We use essential cookies so the session works. No advertising cookies or cross-site tracking.
legal.s6p2: You can ask us to delete your data at any time by writing to us. We do it, no questions asked.
legal.s7p1: For any question about your data, a bid, or anything — write to us at mancha.gallery@gmail.com. We reply promptly.
legal.footerNote1: This document is a plain explanation intended for an early stage of MANCHA. It does not replace formal legal advice.

notasPage.heroSub: Art, history, judgement. Five centuries of geniuses, impossible records, museums and facts no one told you. No editorial algorithms.
notasPage.lecturasDesc: Long stories to read at your own pace.
notasPage.artistasDesc: It's not a ranking of taste — it's a map of who changed the rules of the game. Hover over each one.
notasPage.carosTitle: The most expensive paintings in the world
notasPage.carosDesc: Figures in millions of dollars. The market prices the story as much as the work.
notasPage.datosDesc: Small stories that make art more human.
notasPage.museosTitle: Museums to see once in a lifetime
notasPage.museosDesc: Where the art that changed history lives.
notasPage.premiosDesc: The recognition that marks a career.
notasPage.cierrePre: All this history began the same way
notasPage.cierreSub: The names in this atlas were once emerging artists no one knew. The next page of this story is being written now.

council.beat1a: MANCHA is assembling its Founding Curatorial Council.
council.beat1b: A few names. Chosen by hand.
council.beat2a: Every work is judged blind — no name, no country, no résumé.
council.beat2b: The work first. The artist after.
council.beat3a: This isn't a job, and it isn't a favor.
council.beat3c: one that enters MANCHA's permanent record.
council.formSub: This is not an open call. We read every voice with care — and if it fits, we reach out.
council.focusPlaceholder: Curation, criticism, museums, the art market…
council.statementPlaceholder: What you look for in a work, and why your judgment counts.
council.success: Your application is in. If it fits, we'll be in touch.
council.back: or get to know MANCHA first
council.step1Sub: The essentials, to get to know you.
council.step3Sub: Choose where your judgment is strongest.
council.step4Sub: Why your eye, and why MANCHA.
council.availabilityPlaceholder: How many reviews could you take per season?
council.proof1Body: The work first: no name, no country, no résumé.
council.proof2Body: Three independent eyes shape every decision.
council.proof3Body: Your judgment enters MANCHA's institutional archive.
council.errorMsg: We couldn't submit your application. Check your details and try again.

curatorsPage.metaDesc: Who selects at MANCHA: a curatorial council that judges every work blind. The work first.
curatorsPage.sub: Behind every MANCHA selection there’s a curatorial council. It judges each work blind —no name, no country, no résumé— so the only thing that weighs is the work.
curatorsPage.p1Body: Each work is judged without knowing who made it. The work first; the artist after.
curatorsPage.p2Body: Three independent eyes shape every decision. No one decides alone.
curatorsPage.p3Body: Every judgment is kept in MANCHA’s institutional archive.
curatorsPage.councilSub: Curators, critics and historians who lend their eye to MANCHA’s standard.
curatorsPage.empty: The council is being formed. We’ll introduce its members soon.
curatorsPage.founderBody: Whoever leads MANCHA chooses not to appear. It’s consistent with everything else: let the work speak, not the name — not even the name of who presents it.
curatorsPage.ctaExpertsTitle: Are you a curator or critic?
curatorsPage.ctaExpertsBody: The council grows by invitation. If your eye can add something, introduce yourself.

onboarding.errorAcuerdos: To continue, accept the Code of Ethics and the Collaboration Agreement.
onboarding.s1FounderMsg: Thank you for agreeing to look with us. At MANCHA the work speaks first; your judgment builds the standard that holds it. We don’t expect perfection: we expect honesty, criterion and care.
onboarding.s1Body: This onboarding takes a few minutes. We’ll agree on how we work, set up your profile, and show you how the portal works. After that, you’ll have full access to the curatorial room.
onboarding.s2Intro: The council rests on trust. By joining, you commit to these principles:
onboarding.s2p1: Judge every work blind, without seeking or using the artist’s identity during Phase 1.
onboarding.s2p2: Give honest, well-founded judgments, free of favoritism, pressure or personal interest.
onboarding.s2p3: Declare any conflict of interest and recuse yourself when one exists.
onboarding.s2p4: Keep confidentiality over the works, the deliberations and the council’s data.
onboarding.s2p5: Respect artists, colleagues and the process, even in disagreement.
onboarding.s2Accept: I have read and accept the Code of Ethics.
onboarding.s3Intro: A simple understanding of how we work together:
onboarding.s3p1: You take part by invitation, as a member of the Founding Curatorial Council, with no employment relationship.
onboarding.s3p2: Your evaluations are sealed and immutable; the record is permanent and auditable.
onboarding.s3p3: The final decision on each work rests with the direction, informed by the council.
onboarding.s3p4: You may step away at any time; your prior contribution stays in the institutional archive.
onboarding.s3Accept: I have read and accept the Collaboration Agreement.
onboarding.s4Sub: This is how we present you to the council and, if you choose, to the public.
onboarding.s4BioPh: A sentence or two about your eye and your background.
onboarding.s5Sub: Where your judgment is strongest and how much you can take on.
onboarding.s5AvailPh: How many reviews could you take per season?
onboarding.s6Sub: A quick look before you go in.
onboarding.s6t1Body: You judge the work without knowing who made it. The work first; the artist after.
onboarding.s6t2Body: You score with weighted criteria, your confidence level and a reflection.
onboarding.s6t3Body: Three eyes meet; the direction decides, informed by the council.
onboarding.s6t4Body: Your contribution is recorded and you can download your certificate.
